Q10_order.operationalization: full chronological order (Nöldeke/Egyptian) is external to the corpus and prohibited; only the intrinsic meccan/medinan partition is available
Q10_order.question: Q10 revelation order vs canonical order — which explains the structure better
Q10_order.status: UNKNOWN (revelation order is EXTERNAL data — forbidden)
Q10_order.structural_fact: meccan and medinan partitions show near-identical residual structure; the canonical order is the only intrinsic order, and chronological order cannot be tested without external data
Q10_order.verdict: UNKNOWN (chronology forbidden); meccan/medinan differ only marginally in residual

Q11.operationalization: verb aspect PERF(completed/past) vs IMPF(ongoing) vs IMPV(command); FUT particle; T time-nouns; recurrence as a cyclic-vs-linear proxy
Q11.question: Q11 how the Quran models time
Q11.structural_answer: Near-balance of completed (47.3%) and ongoing (43.0%) aspect with a heavy imperative load and pervasive recurrence — time is modelled as RECURRENT/aspectual (completed exemplar ↔ present address), not as a single linear chronology

Q12.operationalization: mean root-Jaccard of mirror-symmetric ayah pairs (i, n-1-i) within surahs vs a within-surah ayah-order shuffle null (200 reps)
Q12.question: Q12 does a geometric (ring/symmetry) architecture exist
Q12.structural_answer: Mirror-symmetric ayah pairs are slightly more lexically similar than chance ordering (z=-1.46) — NO ring/symmetry signal
Q12.verdict: NO geometric architecture beyond chance ordering

Q13.operationalization: greedy removal of lowest document-frequency roots; the last-surviving units are the highest-frequency hub (Arabic anchors are evidence)
Q13.question: Q13 what survives longest under deletion (essentiality)
Q13.structural_answer: The essential core that survives deletion longest IS the high-frequency hub — essentiality reduces to frequency dominance (the known invariant).
Q13.verdict: FREQUENCY HUB (essentiality ≡ frequency dominance, not a separate structure)

Q14.operationalization: (a) roots most over-represented in INTG interrogative ayahs (lift); (b) maximal discourse allocation = highest df + co-occurrence degree. Arabic anchors are evidence, not glosses.
Q14.question: Q14 which question does the Quran spend most effort answering
Q14.structural_answer: The maximal-allocation centre is the frequency hub; the interrogative structure points at the same high-allocation anchors. The 'central question' is not recoverable as semantics — it reduces to the central anchor's discourse allocation (evidence: the listed Arabic anchors).
Q14.verdict: UNKNOWN as semantics; structurally = maximal discourse allocation on the central anchor (frequency hub)

Q1_minimization.operationalization: no structural test can rank semantic candidates (error/uncertainty/conflict/…) without imposed meaning; the only corpus-internal reading is information-theoretic (description length) + what is grammatically negated
Q1_minimization.question: Q1 what does the Quran minimize

structural_proxy.defensible_answer: lexical description length — the text minimizes coding cost of its frequent forms (Zipfian compression); redundancy = 0.203918

Q2_maximization.operationalization: same blocker as Q1; defensible reading = redundancy / repetition of the highest-allocation anchors (maximal coverage by few hubs)
Q2_maximization.question: Q2 what does the Quran maximize
Q2_maximization.verdict: UNKNOWN (semantic); structurally = redundancy / repetition of central anchors

Q3.operationalization: person frame from features_raw: 1st/2nd person = human address; 3rd person = world description
Q3.question: Q3 human-model vs world-model
Q3.structural_answer: HUMAN-ADDRESSED WORLD-MODEL: a 3rd-person world (59.5%) narrated to a 2nd/1st-person human addressee (40.5%); the I–you address frame is far heavier than neutral narration
Q3.verdict: BOTH — world-content in a human-address frame (not reducible to one)

Q5_hidden_reality.operationalization: distinguishing observable/inferable/hidden requires world knowledge the corpus-only method forbids; only the reference frame is structural
Q5_hidden_reality.question: Q5 observable vs inferable vs structurally-hidden reference
Q5_hidden_reality.structural_fact: reference is predominantly 3rd-person (a domain outside the I–you discourse), but observability cannot be decided structurally

Q6_omission.operationalization: structural silence proxy = pronoun(PRON)/anaphora load relative to content nouns; high pronoun-to-noun ratio = unresolved reference
Q6_omission.question: Q6 what is systematically left unsaid (omission architecture)
Q6_omission.structural_fact: substantial pronominal/anaphoric reference (~0.13 per noun) is left to resolution outside the local ayah — structural silence is real but its *content* is unrecoverable
Q6_omission.verdict: PARTIAL — omission is structurally present (anaphora) but its content is UNKNOWN

Q7.operationalization: token mass by POS class (object/entity nominal vs relational/structural particle+verb) and graph node:edge ratio
Q7.question: Q7 object-priority vs relation-priority
Q7.structural_answer: Relations carry slightly more token mass (51.3% vs 48.7% objects) AND the co-occurrence graph is strongly edge-dense (45.2 edges/node) — relations dominate both the surface and the structure
Q7.verdict: RELATIONS are more fundamental — both in token mass (51%%) and in graph density (45 edges/node)

Q8.operationalization: verb aspect: PERF=static/descriptive (book); IMPF+IMPV=process (engine); plus COND conditionals and IMPV commands as process triggers
Q8.question: Q8 knowledge-repository (book) vs process-generator (engine)
Q8.structural_answer: Process aspect 52.7% of finite verbs, plus 1876 imperatives and 1049 conditionals — the text is markedly an ENGINE (commands + if/then) more than a static repository
Q8.verdict: ENGINE-leaning: process aspect + heavy imperative/conditional load

Q9.operationalization: remove all POS=PN proper-noun tokens; recompute frequency-skew, residual, and coherence-beyond-null at root level
Q9.question: Q9 how much survives name removal
Q9.structural_answer: Proper nouns are 5.0% of tokens; removing them leaves frequency-skew, residual, and coherence essentially unchanged — structure is name-INDEPENDENT
Q9.verdict: MOST survives (~95%% of tokens are not names; frequency/residual/coherence invariants unchanged)
